SEARCH
Package io.github.senthilganeshs.fj.ds

Interface Collection<T>

Type Parameters:
T - The type of elements in the collection.
All Known Subinterfaces:
Array<T>, Deque<T>, Either<A,B>, Graph<V>, HashMap<K,V>, LazyList<T>, List<T>, Maybe<T>, PriorityQueue<T>, Queue<T>, RoseTree<T>, Set<T>, Set.AVLTree<T>, Stack<T>, Validation<E,T>, Vector<T>
All Known Implementing Classes:
Array.NonEmpty, Deque.BankersDeque, Either.Left, Either.Right, Graph.AdjacencyMapGraph, HashMap.HAMT, LazyList.Empty, LazyList.NonEmpty, List.EmptyList, List.LinkedList, Maybe.Nothing, Maybe.Some, PriorityQueue.LeftistHeap, Queue.BankersQueue, RoseTree.RoseTreeImpl, Set.Empty, Set.NonEmpty, Stack.Empty, Stack.NonEmpty, Validation.Invalid, Validation.Valid, Vector.VectorImpl
public interface Collection<T>
The fundamental abstraction for all functional data structures in this library.

To implement a new data structure, only three methods are required: empty(), build(Object), and foldl(Object, BiFunction). All other functional methods are provided as defaults based on this core triad.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default boolean
    all(Predicate<T> pred)
    Returns true if all elements satisfy the predicate.
    default boolean
    any(Predicate<T> pred)
    Returns true if any element satisfies the predicate.
    default <R> Collection<R>
    apply(Collection<Function<T,R>> fns)
    Applicative apply: applies a collection of functions to this collection of values.
    static <T> AffineTraversal<Collection<T>,T>
    at(int index)
    Returns an AffineTraversal that focuses on a single element at the specified index.
    Collection<T>
    build(T input)
    Creates a new instance of the data structure with the provided element added.
    default Collection<Collection<T>>
    chunk(int size)
    Splits the collection into sub-collections of the specified size.
    default Collection<T>
    concat(Collection<T> first)
    Combines two collections by appending the provided collection to the end of this one.
    default int
    count()
    Alias for length().
    default Collection<T>
    distinct()
    Returns a new collection containing only unique elements using standard equals.
    default Collection<T>
    distinct(Eq<T> eq)
    Returns a new collection containing only unique elements using the provided Eq strategy.
    default Collection<T>
    drop(int n)
    Drops the first n elements from the collection.
    default Collection<T>
    dropWhile(Predicate<T> pred)
    Drops elements as long as the predicate is satisfied.
    static <T> Traversal<Collection<T>,T>
    eachP()
    Returns a Traversal that focuses on every element in a collection.
    <R> Collection<R>
    empty()
    Returns an empty instance of the data structure.
    default Collection<T>
    filter(Predicate<T> pred)
    Returns a new collection containing only elements that satisfy the predicate.
    default <R> Collection<R>
    filterType(Class<R> clazz)
    Filters elements by their class type and casts them.
    default Maybe<T>
    find(Predicate<T> pred)
    Finds the first element satisfying the predicate.
    default Maybe<Integer>
    findIndex(Predicate<T> pred)
    Finds the index of the first element satisfying the predicate.
    default <R> Collection<R>
    flatMap(Function<T,Collection<R>> fn)
    Maps each element to a collection and flattens the result into a single collection.
    static <S, R extends Collection<S>>
    Collection<S>
    flatten(Collection<R> rs)
     
    default T
    fold(Monoid<T> monoid)
     
    <R> R
    foldl(R seed, BiFunction<R,T,R> fn)
    Performs a left-associative reduction (fold) over the elements of this collection.
    default <R> R
    foldMap(Function<T,R> fn, Monoid<R> monoid)
     
    default <R> R
    foldr(R seed, BiFunction<T,R,R> fn)
    Performs a right-associative reduction over the elements of this collection.
    default Collection<T>
    forEach(Consumer<T> action)
    Performs an action for each element in the collection.
    default <K> HashMap<K,Collection<T>>
    groupBy(Function<T,K> keyFn)
    Categorizes elements into a HashMap based on a key extraction function.
    default Maybe<T>
    headMaybe()
    Returns the first element of the collection safely.
    default Maybe<Integer>
    indexOf(T value)
    Finds the index of the first occurrence of the provided value.
    default Collection<Collection<T>>
    intercalate(Collection<Collection<T>> rss)
    Intersperses this collection between every two collections in the input collection.
    default Collection<T>
    intersperse(T sep)
    Places the separator element between every two existing elements.
    default Maybe<T>
    lastMaybe()
    Returns the last element of the collection safely.
    default int
    length()
    Returns the total number of elements in the collection.

    map(Function<T,R> fn)
    Transforms each element of the collection using the provided function.
    default <R> Collection<R>
    mapMaybe(Function<T,Maybe<R>> fn)
    Maps each element to a Maybe-producing function and keeps only the "Some" results.
    default String
    mkString(String sep)
    Renders the collection as a string with a separator.

    mkString(String start, String sep, String end)
    Renders the collection as a string with customizable boundaries and separator.

    Splits the collection into two collections based on a predicate.
    default Maybe<T>

    default Collection<T>

    Reverses the order of elements in the collection.

    Like foldl, but returns a collection of all intermediate accumulation states.

    Returns a view of a portion of this collection.

  • Method Details

    • empty

      <R> Collection<R> empty()
      Returns an empty instance of the data structure.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of elements for the empty collection.
      Returns:
      An empty Collection implementation.

    • build

      Collection<T> build(T input)
      Creates a new instance of the data structure with the provided element added. This operation is persistent and does not modify the original instance.
      Parameters:
      input - The element to add.
      Returns:
      A new Collection containing the added element.

    • foldl

      <R> R foldl(R seed, BiFunction<R,T,R> fn)
      Performs a left-associative reduction (fold) over the elements of this collection. This is the primary traversal mechanism for all derived operations.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of the accumulated result.
      Parameters:
      seed - The initial value for the accumulation.
      fn - The combining function (accumulator, element) -> result.
      Returns:
      The final accumulated value.

    • foldr

      default <R> R foldr(R seed, BiFunction<T,R,R> fn)
      Performs a right-associative reduction over the elements of this collection.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of the accumulated result.
      Parameters:
      seed - The initial value for the accumulation.
      fn - The combining function (element, accumulator) -> result.
      Returns:
      The final accumulated value.

    • map

      default <R> Collection<R> map(Function<T,R> fn)
      Transforms each element of the collection using the provided function.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of elements in the resulting collection.
      Parameters:
      fn - The transformation function.
      Returns:
      A new collection containing the transformed elements.

    • traverse

      default <R> Collection<Collection<R>> traverse(Function<T,Collection<R>> fn)
      Maps each element to a collection-producing function, then flips the structure. Also known as "inside-out" mapping.

      Example: List.of(1, 2).traverse(i -> Maybe.some(i + 1)) results in Some([2, 3])

      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of elements in the inner collection.
      Parameters:
      fn - Function that returns a collection.
      Returns:
      A collection of collections.

    • mapMaybe

      default <R> Collection<R> mapMaybe(Function<T,Maybe<R>> fn)
      Maps each element to a Maybe-producing function and keeps only the "Some" results.
      Type Parameters:
      R - Resulting element type.
      Parameters:
      fn - Transformation function.
      Returns:
      A collection of successful results.

    • filter

      default Collection<T> filter(Predicate<T> pred)
      Returns a new collection containing only elements that satisfy the predicate.
      Parameters:
      pred - The condition to test against.
      Returns:
      A filtered collection.

    • filterType

      default <R> Collection<R> filterType(Class<R> clazz)
      Filters elements by their class type and casts them. Useful for heterogeneous collections.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The target type.
      Parameters:
      clazz - The class to filter by.
      Returns:
      A collection containing only elements of type R.

    • find

      default Maybe<T> find(Predicate<T> pred)
      Finds the first element satisfying the predicate.
      Parameters:
      pred - The condition to search for.
      Returns:
      Some(element) if found, Nothing otherwise.

    • findIndex

      default Maybe<Integer> findIndex(Predicate<T> pred)
      Finds the index of the first element satisfying the predicate.
      Parameters:
      pred - The condition to test.
      Returns:
      Some(index) if found, Nothing otherwise.

    • indexOf

      default Maybe<Integer> indexOf(T value)
      Finds the index of the first occurrence of the provided value.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to search for.
      Returns:
      Some(index) if found, Nothing otherwise.

    • liftA2

      default <R, S> Collection<S> liftA2(Function<T,Function<R,S>> fn, Collection<R> rs)

    • liftA2

      default <R, S> Collection<S> liftA2(BiFunction<T,R,S> fn, Collection<R> rs)

    • liftA3

      default <R, S, U> Collection<U> liftA3(Function<T,Function<R,Function<S,U>>> fn, Collection<R> rs, Collection<S> ss)

    • liftA4

      default <R, S, U, V> Collection<V> liftA4(Function<T,Function<R,Function<S,Function<U,V>>>> fn, Collection<R> rs, Collection<S> ss, Collection<U> us)

    • concat

      default Collection<T> concat(Collection<T> first)
      Combines two collections by appending the provided collection to the end of this one.
      Parameters:
      first - The collection to append.
      Returns:
      A new collection containing elements of both.

    • flatMap

      default <R> Collection<R> flatMap(Function<T,Collection<R>> fn)
      Maps each element to a collection and flattens the result into a single collection.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of elements in the resulting collection.
      Parameters:
      fn - The transformation function.
      Returns:
      A flattened collection of results.

    • partition

      default Tuple<Collection<T>,Collection<T>> partition(Predicate<T> pred)
      Splits the collection into two collections based on a predicate. The first collection contains elements that satisfy the predicate, the second contains those that do not.
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate condition.
      Returns:
      A Tuple containing (satisfied, dissatisfied).

    • zipWithIndex

      default Collection<Tuple<T,Integer>> zipWithIndex()
      Pairs each element with its 0-based index.
      Returns:
      A collection of Tuples (element, index).

    • zipWith

      default <R, S> Collection<S> zipWith(BiFunction<T,R,S> fn, List<R> other)
      Combines two collections element-wise using the provided function.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of elements in the other collection.
      S - The type of elements in the resulting collection.
      Parameters:
      fn - The combining function.
      other - The other collection.
      Returns:
      A collection of combined results.

    • apply

      default <R> Collection<R> apply(Collection<Function<T,R>> fns)
      Applicative apply: applies a collection of functions to this collection of values.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The resulting element type.
      Parameters:
      fns - The collection of functions.
      Returns:
      A collection containing every possible application of the functions to the values.

    • forEach

      default Collection<T> forEach(Consumer<T> action)
      Performs an action for each element in the collection.
      Parameters:
      action - The consumer action.
      Returns:
      This collection (for chaining).

    • groupBy

      default <K> HashMap<K,Collection<T>> groupBy(Function<T,K> keyFn)
      Categorizes elements into a HashMap based on a key extraction function.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The type of the key.
      Parameters:
      keyFn - The key extraction function.
      Returns:
      A HashMap where each key points to a collection of elements belonging to that group.

    • scanl

      default <R> Collection<R> scanl(R seed, BiFunction<R,T,R> fn)
      Like foldl, but returns a collection of all intermediate accumulation states.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of states.
      Parameters:
      seed - The initial state.
      fn - The accumulation function.
      Returns:
      A collection of states.

    • span

      default Tuple<Collection<T>,Collection<T>> span(Predicate<T> pred)
      Splits the collection into two parts based on a predicate. The first part contains the prefix of elements satisfying the predicate. The second part contains the remaining elements.
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate condition.
      Returns:
      A Tuple containing (takeWhile, dropWhile).

    • chunk

      default Collection<Collection<T>> chunk(int size)
      Splits the collection into sub-collections of the specified size.
      Parameters:
      size - The size of each chunk.
      Returns:
      A collection of chunks.

    • length

      default int length()
      Returns the total number of elements in the collection.
      Returns:
      The size of the collection.

    • drop

      default Collection<T> drop(int n)
      Drops the first n elements from the collection.
      Parameters:
      n - Number of elements to drop.
      Returns:
      A collection containing all but the first n elements.

    • reverse

      default Collection<T> reverse()
      Reverses the order of elements in the collection.
      Returns:
      A reversed collection.

    • takeWhile

      default Collection<T> takeWhile(Predicate<T> pred)
      Takes elements as long as the predicate is satisfied.
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate condition.
      Returns:
      A collection containing the prefix elements satisfying the predicate.

    • dropWhile

      default Collection<T> dropWhile(Predicate<T> pred)
      Drops elements as long as the predicate is satisfied.
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate condition.
      Returns:
      A collection containing the remaining elements after dropping the prefix.

    • take

      default Collection<T> take(int n)
      Takes the first n elements from the collection.
      Parameters:
      n - Number of elements to take.
      Returns:
      A collection containing only the first n elements.

    • intersperse

      default Collection<T> intersperse(T sep)
      Places the separator element between every two existing elements.
      Parameters:
      sep - The separator.
      Returns:
      A collection with separators interspersed.

    • intercalate

      default Collection<Collection<T>> intercalate(Collection<Collection<T>> rss)
      Intersperses this collection between every two collections in the input collection.
      Parameters:
      rss - a collection of collections.
      Returns:
      a single collection with elements of this collection intercalated.

    • slice

      default Collection<T> slice(int start, int n)
      Returns a view of a portion of this collection.
      Parameters:
      start - The starting index.
      n - The number of elements.
      Returns:
      A slice of the collection.

    • count

      default int count()
      Alias for length().
      Returns:
      The element count.

    • any

      default boolean any(Predicate<T> pred)
      Returns true if any element satisfies the predicate.
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate condition.
      Returns:
      True if at least one match is found.

    • all

      default boolean all(Predicate<T> pred)
      Returns true if all elements satisfy the predicate.
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate condition.
      Returns:
      True if all elements match.

    • headMaybe

      default Maybe<T> headMaybe()
      Returns the first element of the collection safely.
      Returns:
      Some(head) if not empty, Nothing otherwise.

    • lastMaybe

      default Maybe<T> lastMaybe()
      Returns the last element of the collection safely.
      Returns:
      Some(last) if not empty, Nothing otherwise.

    • reduce

      default Maybe<T> reduce(BiFunction<T,T,T> fn)
      Reduces the collection to a single value using a binary function.
      Parameters:
      fn - The reduction function.
      Returns:
      Some(result) if not empty, Nothing otherwise.

    • mkString

      default String mkString(String start, String sep, String end)
      Renders the collection as a string with customizable boundaries and separator.
      Parameters:
      start - Starting string.
      sep - Separator string.
      end - Ending string.
      Returns:
      The rendered string.

    • mkString

      default String mkString(String sep)
      Renders the collection as a string with a separator.
      Parameters:
      sep - Separator string.
      Returns:
      The rendered string.

    • distinct

      default Collection<T> distinct()
      Returns a new collection containing only unique elements using standard equals.
      Returns:
      A collection without duplicates.

    • distinct

      default Collection<T> distinct(Eq<T> eq)
      Returns a new collection containing only unique elements using the provided Eq strategy.
      Parameters:
      eq - The equality strategy.
      Returns:
      A collection without duplicates.

    • flatten

      static <S, R extends Collection<S>> Collection<S> flatten(Collection<R> rs)

    • sequence

      static <R> Collection<Collection<R>> sequence(Collection<Collection<R>> rs)

    • sum

      static <R extends Number> double sum(Collection<R> rs)

    • fold

      default T fold(Monoid<T> monoid)

    • foldMap

      default <R> R foldMap(Function<T,R> fn, Monoid<R> monoid)

    • unfold

      static <T, S> Collection<T> unfold(S seed, Function<S,Maybe<Tuple<T,S>>> f)

    • eachP

      static <T> Traversal<Collection<T>,T> eachP()
      Returns a Traversal that focuses on every element in a collection.

    • at

      static <T> AffineTraversal<Collection<T>,T> at(int index)
      Returns an AffineTraversal that focuses on a single element at the specified index.
